
---
title: "Results cadmium transfer to liver and kidney"
output: word_document
params:
  age: NA
  feed: NA
  intake1: NA
  intake2: NA
  intake3: NA
  concCad: NA
  expDuration:  NA
  deplTime: NA
  selected_organ: NA
---

`r format(Sys.Date(), "%d %B %Y")`   

## Input parameters used in the simulation  
* __Contamimated feed product:__ `r params$feed`.   
* __Intake of the feed product:__  
    + `r params$intake1` kg dm/day (<15 weeks), 
    + `r params$intake2` kg dm/day (15-20 weeks),
    + `r params$intake3` kg dm/day (>20 weeks)    
* __Cadmium level:__ `r params$concCad` mg/kg feed.    
* __Age at the start of the exposure period:__ `r params$age` weeks.    
* __Exposure duration:__ `r params$expDuration` weeks.    
* __Depletion time:__ `r params$deplTime` weeks.    

## Simulation results
```{r echo=FALSE, warning = FALSE}
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # code of cadmium in pigs model, re-implemented from ASCL into R
  # fixed dose-regime, but time of stopping the regime is user-defined
  # dose size is fixed, but has to be made user-defined
  # implementation follows previous model implementations
  # (1) using R ode-procedure to solve DE
  # (2) function that defines gradient for given time-point and values of state-variables 
  # (3) ordered series of time points and related event indicator values
  #-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  norg		<- 2
  orgnames	<- c("Kidney", "Liver")
  dayw		<- 7
  
  #-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # general parameters
  #-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  Cback <- 0.04				#User defined in ShinyApp: mg Cd/kg feed ; Cstart	<- 0.89; Cgrow <- 0.86
  #User defined in ShinyApp:  Fstart	<- dayw * 1.4; Fgrow1 <- dayw * 2.; Fgrow2 <- dayw *  2.6	#kg feed/d
  Tgrow1	<- 3.; Tgrow2 <- 8. #ser defined in ShinyApp: ; Tdoseoff <- 16;				
  #tSTOP		<- 15.
  
  alfakl	<- c(.051, .059)
  betakl	<- dayw * c(12.4, 37.4)
  
  MT0kl		<- c(8.65, 22.3)
  dMTdCkl	<- c(.923, 0)
  V0kl		<- c(.114, .626)
  dVdtkl	<- c(.0256, .100)					# weight growth parameters
  
  ML<- c(1.,.5)
  
  names(alfakl) <- names(betakl) <- names(MT0kl) <- names(dMTdCkl) <- names(V0kl) <- names(dVdtkl) <-
    orgnames
  
  A0	<- 0.							#mg Cd
  
  #-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # DERIVATIVE FUNCTION USED FOR SOLVING DIFFERENTIAL EQUATIONS
  #-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
f.fun <- function(t, y, pars) {
    with(as.list(c(y, pars)), {
    ifelse(t<params$expDuration, expon <- 1, expon <- 0)
    V	<- V0 + dVdt * t
    Cd	<- A / V
    MT	<- MT0 + dMTdC * Cd
    A	<- alfa * MT * D / (hbeta + D)  
    D	<- -D +((1 - expon) * Cback*params$intake3*dayw)
    res<-c(A,V,D)
    result<-(list(res, output= Cd))}
      )
  }
  
  
  #-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # SOLVING DIFFERENTIAL EQUATIONS FOR GIVEN ORGAN, TIME-OF-STOPPING-DOSE, AND ORGAN NAME
  #-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  # note: all parameters used within the derivative function are defined globally
  
  f.model0 <-  function(i) {
    alfa		<<- alfakl[i]
    hbeta		<<- betakl[i]
    MT0		<<- MT0kl[i]
    dMTdC		<<- dMTdCkl[i]
    V0		<<- V0kl[i]
    dVdt		<<- dVdtkl[i]
  }

  f.model1 <- function(pars, orgname) {
    yini<-c(A=0, V=0, D=0)
    tout<-seq(params$age-12, params$expDuration+params$deplTime+params$age-12, by = 1)
    toutTrue<-seq(params$age, params$expDuration+params$deplTime+params$age, by = 1)
    feedPeriod1Fixed<-data.frame(var = c("D") ,time = seq(0,3, by =1), value = c(params$intake1*dayw* params$concCad), method = c("add"))
    feedPeriod1Flex<-data.frame(var = c("D") ,time = seq(0,params$expDuration, by =1), value = c(params$intake1*dayw* params$concCad), method = c("add"))
    feedPeriod2Fixed<-data.frame(var = c("D") ,time = seq(0,8, by =1), value = c(params$intake2*dayw* params$concCad), method = c("add"))
    feedPeriod2Flex<-data.frame(var = c("D") ,time = seq(0,params$expDuration, by =1), value = c(params$intake2*dayw* params$concCad), method = c("add"))
    feedPeriod3Flex<-data.frame(var = c("D") ,time = seq(0,params$expDuration, by =1), value = c(params$intake3*dayw* params$concCad), method = c("add"))
    if ( params$expDuration<=3) {
      dosing<- feedPeriod1Flex 
    } else if ( params$expDuration>3 & params$expDuration <=8) {
      dosing<- rbind(feedPeriod1Fixed,feedPeriod2Flex)%>% slice(., -5:-8)
    } else {
      dosing<- rbind(feedPeriod1Fixed,feedPeriod2Fixed, feedPeriod3Flex) %>% slice(., -5:-8)%>% slice(., -10:-18)
    }
    
    hf	<- as.data.frame(ode(y = yini, times = tout, func = f.fun, events = list(data=dosing), parms = NULL))
    
    # Find points where concentration is above legal limit.
    # Find points where concentration is above legal limit.
    EUlimit <- ML[as.numeric(params$selected_organ)]
    above<-hf$output>EUlimit
    # Points always intersect when above=TRUE, then FALSE or reverse
    intersect.points<-which(diff(above)!=0 & above == TRUE)-params$expDuration
    # Find peak concentration
    cmax<-max(hf$output)
    cD2<-max(hf$output[2])
    if (length(intersect.points) == 0  & cmax < EUlimit )  {
      intersect.text<-paste("(tissue concentration below EU limit of", as.character(EUlimit),"mg/kg)")
    } else if (length(intersect.points) == 0 & cmax >= EUlimit) { 
      intersect.text<- "(EU limit not reached at end of depletion time)"
    } else if (cD2>EUlimit & intersect.points <= params$expDuration+1 | intersect.points==params$deplTime+1){  
      intersect.text<- "(EU limit not reached at end of depletion time)"
    } else {
      intersect.text<-paste("EU limit reached after", as.character(intersect.points), "days from last exposure")
    }
    
    xlab	<- "Time (weeks)"
    ylab	<- paste("Cd concentration (mg/kg", orgname, ")")
    plot(toutTrue, hf$output, type = "l", xlab = xlab, ylab = ylab)

  }
  
  f.model <- function(i, Tdoseoff, orgname) {
    f.model0(i)
    f.model1(Tdoseoff, orgname)
  }
  
	f.model(1, Tgrow1, orgnames[1])

	
	f.model(2, Tgrow2, orgnames[2])

  
```
